title: Deliver a security-hardened papercuts fork
description:
## Outcome
Evolve the public 39elbarto/papercuts fork from upstream v0.1.0 into a security-hardened, agent-first CLI that can be adopted across many repositories without silently violating read-only boundaries, dirtying every worktree, or publishing sensitive context.

## Product constraints
Preserve the upstream append-only JSONL journal, deterministic machine contract, structured stdout/stderr, stable exit codes, concurrent writers, doctor diagnostics, and no-network logging. Prefer small upstream-friendly changes over a rewrite. No server, telemetry, automatic fixes, raw transcript storage, or ACFS-specific logic in the generic Rust core.

## Completion definition
All child decisions are recorded; safe single-project logging and review are implemented and adversarially verified; an allowlisted pilot produces evidence; multi-project review is implemented only after pilot approval; public release gates, attribution, compatibility, rollback, and secret scans pass. Each child bead must be independently executable from its own description and evidence requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Все обязательные дочерние phase gates закрыты доказательствами; публичный release воспроизводим и не содержит приватных данных; append-only и machine-contract инварианты сохранены; rollback проверен.
notes: Polished through seven passes on 2026-07-11: full plan conversion; dependency graph; structured acceptance and estimates; missing follow-up coverage; priority/self-containment repair; implementation ownership and test/evidence review; final br/bv steady-state audit. Exactly one child is ready: br-hardened-papercuts-fork-x30.1.

title: Inventory the upstream v0.1.0 contract and compatibility surface
description:
## Background
The fork starts from treygoff24/papercuts v0.1.0. Upstream treats docs/plans/2026-07-09-papercuts-design.md as normative and promises append-only JSONL, stable envelopes, contract version 1, deterministic IDs, bounded locks, explicit mutation annotations, and specific exit codes. Hardening must not silently invalidate those guarantees.

## Work
Trace CLI flags, environment discovery, record shapes, schema output, path capture, error mapping, read-only behavior, file creation, locking, fold semantics, and existing tests. Produce a compatibility matrix distinguishing unchanged upstream behavior, intended breaking changes, additive changes, and fork-only policy. Record which changes require contract-version movement. Inspect https://github.com/treygoff24/papercuts/issues/1 in full: committed-by-default sensitive-data/path leakage, undocumented effective Rust MSRV, the dangling absolute local skill symlink, and .git-marker repository detection. Classify each as generic upstream fix, fork hardening, documentation-only, or rejected with rationale.

## Evidence and tests
Use exact source/test references and representative command output. Run the unchanged upstream build, test, clippy, fmt, and doctor gate. Do not modify product behavior in this bead.

## Acceptance
A future implementer can understand every public compatibility boundary without rereading the original plan; unknowns are explicit; no implementation begins; evidence contains no secrets or unnecessary private paths.
acceptance_criteria: Матрица покрывает CLI, env/discovery, records, schema, errors, locking, side effects, tests и все четыре пункта upstream issue #1; baseline gate зелёный; продуктовый код не изменён.
notes:
Evidence: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md
Baseline: release build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS; fmt PASS; papercuts doctor healthy with 2 lines.
Live upstream: main ffba2bd453ab0faeadf4f923fc727586958c8d6f; v0.1.0 5d8b827abbd054f5f506d26be865f5b7f573a298; issue #1 open without comments.
Scope proof: no diff under src/, tests/, Cargo.toml, or Cargo.lock.
Docs: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md and docs/WORKLOG.md.
close_reason: Completed upstream v0.1.0 contract inventory and issue #1 classification. Product code unchanged; baseline gate passed. Docs: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md, docs/WORKLOG.md.

title: Update schema, errors, and compatibility contract for hardening
description:
## Outcome
Publish the exact machine contract 2 through schema, record/output models, errors, metadata, warnings, and compatibility tests after profile/path/content shapes stabilize.

## Work
Make schema the complete self-orientation source for commands, relevant environment, precedence, mutation/create behavior, exact records, content/path policies, strict repository rules, storage/migration states, metadata, warnings, all error/exit codes, ID inputs, known limitations, and mixed contract-1/2 reading. Keep error/category constants single-sourced. Sanitize parser/config errors so rejected argv/env values are never echoed. Keep stdout=data, stderr=errors, with only Markdown/help/version plaintext exceptions.

## Tests
Pin semantic schema assertions and representative JSON instances; exact private/committed/resolve records; contract exactly 2; metadata inclusion/omission by command/profile; lexically sorted warnings and set-like arrays; every error/exit/suggested-fix; static schema ignoring ambient env; command-relevant env and lazy clock behavior; unknown-field/v0.1 parse compatibility; private safe errors; committed exposure; mixed-journal behavior.

## Acceptance
The checked-in schema cannot drift from executable constants or behavior; contract 2 and every public shape/error/profile are pinned; safe diagnostics and plaintext exceptions are proven; old records remain readable; full cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Own final schema/error/meta/model convergence after x30.7-.9. Derive dictionaries and category lists from code-level sources of truth. Avoid duplicating pattern constants in docs. Do not alter product behavior merely to simplify schema.
acceptance_criteria: Contract exactly 2; all commands/env/records/meta/warnings/errors/exits/limitations are self-describing and pinned to runtime sources; rejected values and private paths are absent from diagnostics; v0.1 record parse compatibility and cargo/UBS gates pass.
notes:
Storage/path schema input: docs/SAFE_STORAGE_PROFILES_ADR.md and docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. Publish private and committed profiles, separate profile_source default/flag/env from storage_source profile-default/flag-file/env-file, write_policy and path_policy. Cut schema retains cwd String and repo nullable for v1 parsing, adds path_policy omitted or legacy-absolute and path_encoding omitted/utf8/lossy-utf8; exact examples use ID pc_94f5df71022d. Private meta omits file; committed may expose it with warning. Private errors use opaque locations and no raw OS error/path. Missing path_policy infers contract1 legacy; mixed journals read; private output projects safe values; IDs unchanged. Contract exactly 2. New fields are ignored by v1 serde readers and exact safe record passed unchanged v1 list/doctor proof.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Contract/schema input: global --sensitive-policy balanced|strict; add/resolve repeated --allow-sensitive CATEGORY; env PAPERCUTS_SENSITIVE_POLICY and PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE. Add sensitive_input exit 65 with details limited to policy_version, policy, sorted categories and fields. New cut/resolve content_policy is {version,mode,decision clean|warn|override,categories,fields}; clean arrays empty; old missing field means legacy-unscanned; exclude audit from cut ID. Schema must publish category names, bounds, profile floors, precedence, no-off/no-wildcard rules, known misses, and accepted warn/override non-redaction.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Machine contract is exactly 2, not at least 2. Exact new cut order is all v1 fields, path_policy, path_encoding, content_policy; exact shared example ID pc_94f5df71022d. Resolve appends content_policy and folded resolution carries it. Missing path policy means v1 legacy; missing content policy means legacy-unscanned; private output adds omitted path projection but does not invent audit. Add/resolve meta includes contract, storage profile/source, target source, write/path policy, sensitive policy/source/version; list/doctor omit sensitive-policy fields; schema meta is static contract only. Private omits file. All warnings and set-like arrays are sorted/deduped. Publish new errors sensitive_input 65, insecure_private_permissions 77, writes_disabled/storage_required/migration_required/invalid_repository/unsupported_filesystem/unsafe_journal_symlink 78 plus existing codes. Parser/config/ID/since errors never echo rejected argv/env values; private I/O errors use opaque locations. Schema ignores ambient env, documents relevant-env/lazy-clock behavior, all bounds/catalog/limitations, exact warning codes, migration, v0.1 parse-only compatibility and committed divergence.

title: Build adversarial unit and end-to-end security acceptance suite
description:
## Outcome
Create the real-binary adversarial acceptance gate for the complete single-project contract 2. Multi-project inventory/digest behavior is explicitly out of scope until after pilot evidence.

## Coverage
Exercise private and committed profiles, exact flag/env/target/policy precedence, relevant environment and lazy clock, add argv/stdin, all dry-run and read-only paths, storage/migration states, strict Git/worktree/submodule resolution, path projection, every content-policy matrix cell, parser/config redaction, list/resolve/doctor/schema, contract-1/2 mixed and malformed journals, permissions/symlinks/non-UTF8, missing HOME, lock timeout, and distinct/identical concurrent mutations. Use synthetic data and disposable roots only.

## Logging
Provide clearly grouped integration tests or an e2e runner with sanitized preflight, command identity, expected exit/side effects, verification, and cleanup. Never log raw synthetic matched values outside controlled fixture source. On failure retain bounded artifacts named by fixture ID with redacted output and exact reproduction metadata.

## Acceptance
Every single-project contract-2 feature maps to unit and e2e assertions; refused values never persist or echo; private hidden fragments are absent; old-reader/append-only behavior remains; concurrency/store tests run five times when touched; documented logs are sanitized; cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
acceptance_criteria: Real binary passes the complete private/committed contract-2 lifecycle, no-write/refusal, parser redaction, migration, malformed/mixed, permission, worktree, nonUTF, schema and concurrency matrices with sanitized artifacts; multi-project tests remain deferred; cargo/UBS gates pass.
notes:
Path acceptance matrix: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md section 15. Pin exact private and committed bytes and same ID. Scan private journal/stdout/stderr across add duplicate dry-run list resolve already-resolved doctor and errors for every synthetic username/home/repo/common-dir/basename/drive/UNC/symlink fragment. Test private projection of old/mixed records, meta.file absence, explicit-file private vs committed. Exercise ordinary repo, linked worktree, submodule, nested invalid marker, bare/non-Git, gitdir/commondir line grammar, missing HEAD/objects/config, no Git in PATH, symlink rules, traversal, Unix non-UTF8 and legacy lossy warning. Prove v1 parse/doctor compatibility, append-only migration.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Use ADR section 11 as mandatory corpus. Cover all high/medium categories, every persisted field, cross-field AWS pair, balanced/strict/profile-floor behavior, full override state matrix, argv/stdin, CRLF/LF, Unicode/homoglyph controls, invalid UTF-8, exact and plus-one bounds, benign hashes/UUIDs/IDs, exact placeholders, variable references, publishable Stripe keys, AKIA/ASIA alone, multiline config and PEM, encoded/partial-marker misses. For each refusal assert unique sentinel absent from stdout, stderr, filesystem, existing journal delta, debug output and retained artifacts; dry-run has identical decision and zero writes.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. This is single-project contract-2 acceptance only; alias/inventory/digest tests are deferred. Pin exact private/committed/resolve bytes and fixed-clock ID. Cover schema ignoring env; command-relevant textual/native-path env; read-only/config/ID/target/input/scanner/clock/I-O precedence; every storage state and explicit-file migration bypass; private permission and symlink behavior; strict Git grammar; nonUTF paths; private path absence; committed exposure; parser/env no-echo; content corpus and full override matrix; dry-run add no-open and resolve shared-read exceptions; contract1/2/malformed mismatch findings; warning/set ordering; v0.1 parse/doctor proof; races and lock timeout. Failure artifacts identify fixture only and contain no synthetic match. Five-run store/concurrency repetition only when those surfaces change.

title: Publish safe single-project instructions and operator review runbook
description:
## Outcome
Publish a verified, copy-pasteable single-project adoption and operator runbook for the implemented contract-2 binary.

## Work
Update README and canonical AGENTS snippets with private default, explicit committed repository-visible profile, exact CLI/env precedence, read-only task rules, bounded content policy, override prohibition for autonomous agents, path omission and accepted-content limitations, first-run/migration states, doctor/list/resolve lifecycle, evidence cadence, and rollback. Clearly distinguish current implementation from future multi-project work and from upstream v0.1.

## Tests
Run every documented command against the built binary in disposable ordinary/worktree/non-Git cases. Pin snippets or documentation contracts where practical. Use synthetic safe examples; show vendor prefixes only with ellipses. Verify install, preflight, private add, strict refusal, committed warning, list, doctor, resolve, migration copy/verify, no-write task, and rollback output against schema.

## Acceptance
A new user can install/configure, log, review, resolve, migrate, and roll back without hidden knowledge; AGENTS policy does not grant writes or override authority; docs call committed an exposure lane rather than safe/unchecked legacy mode; all commands match schema/runtime; no unsupported security claim remains.
acceptance_criteria: Every documented contract-2 command is verified in disposable repos; AGENTS rules preserve read-only and override authority boundaries; private/committed, migration, limitations and rollback are executable and match schema; no planned or unsupported protection is claimed.
notes:
Path documentation input: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. Explain that private automatic paths are omitted but text, tags, notes and aliases can still disclose names. Show exact safe cwd dot/repo null/path_policy omitted shape and committed legacy-absolute warning. Private outputs redact old path-bearing records but copied source bytes remain; migration does not sanitize Git history. Explicit file does not imply legacy exposure; only committed profile does. Safe errors use opaque locations. Document strict marker/worktree/submodule/symlink/non-UTF8 limits and that path omission is neither encryption nor a cannot-leak guarantee.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Explain guardrail honestly: private balanced warns and persists medium-risk input; committed strict refuses medium and high; detection is incomplete and not redaction. Canonical AGENTS instructions must forbid agents from setting PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE or --allow-sensitive without explicit human authorization for that exact command. Refusal remediation must not print a bypass command. Document exact bounds, policy modes, audit states, no entropy/decoding/Unicode normalization, legacy-unscanned risk, and safe prefix examples with ellipses rather than token-shaped bodies.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Document contract exactly 2 and that the current binary is implemented only after runtime proof. Canonical AGENTS rule: no add/resolve during read-only/audit/no-write tasks, papercut instruction never grants writes, harness may set PAPERCUTS_READ_ONLY=1, continue main task after normal append, and never set PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE or --allow-sensitive without exact human authorization. Explain private implicit common-dir storage, native explicit path risk, committed repository-visible strict exposure, no unchecked mode, migration state/copy/verify/rollback, exact CLI/env relevance, path omission versus accepted text, balanced warnings/overrides persistence, legacy-unscanned risk, scanner misses, parser/diagnostic redaction, and v0.1 parse-only compatibility. Verify every example against built binary and schema; no token-shaped documentation fixtures.

title: Run the hardened single-project release gate
description:
## Outcome
Decide whether the implemented single-project contract 2 is genuinely safe enough for an exact-SHA allowlisted pilot. This is an evidence gate, not a release ceremony.

## Preflight
Confirm the normative ADR, implemented private/committed/path/content/schema behavior, adversarial suite, verified docs, compatibility/migration/rollback, clean worktree, upstream relationship, and fetch-only upstream remote. Record exact upstream/fork SHAs and ahead/behind state without rewriting history.

## Verification
Run release build, all-feature tests, clippy -D warnings, fmt, doctor, contract-2 e2e suite, relevant five-run concurrency sweep, scoped UBS, dependency/license review, and Gitleaks over intended public history. Run the bounded scanner benchmark and manually inspect private/committed stdout, stderr, journal, and artifacts for forbidden path/synthetic sentinel exposure. Exercise v0.1 parsing, migration selection, and rollback. Capture versions, SHA, commands, bounded results, and evidence location.

## Acceptance
Zero unexplained failures, forbidden exposures, or contract drift; every ADR acceptance item traces to commit-pinned evidence; migration/compatibility/rollback work; any waiver is explicit and blocks pilot unless a separately recorded risk decision accepts it.
acceptance_criteria: All contract-2 cargo/e2e/security/performance/compatibility/migration/rollback gates have commit-pinned evidence with zero unexplained failures or forbidden exposures; no waiver silently passes; pilot remains blocked without this gate.
notes:
Release-strategy input copied from x30.6. Before gate, verify origin and upstream parent relationship, fetch-only upstream with DISABLED push sentinel, exact upstream and fork SHAs, ahead and behind counts, and clean public history. Exercise sync on dated branch; merge via merge-commit fork PR and rollback via revert PR. Evaluate name gate: papercuts package and binary are permitted only for exact-SHA isolated pilot; rename before non-isolated distribution if fork-only behavior remains. Prohibit cargo publish as papercuts. Verify MIT attribution, release-note fork disclaimer, tag and version policy, full cargo gate, doctor, gitleaks, and applicable concurrency and UBS evidence. Canonical runbook: docs/UPSTREAM_SYNC_AND_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Release gate must exercise policy v1 corpus, no-echo/no-write refusal assertions, profile floor/downgrade cases, deliberate override audit, mixed legacy-unscanned journals, and max-payload release benchmark >=10000 iterations with p95 <=5ms and max <=20ms on recorded host. Gitleaks remains a release gate, never a runtime dependency.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Gate the exact contract-2 SHA. In addition to existing release evidence, prove schema/runtime match, relevant-env/lazy-clock behavior, fixed-clock exact records/ID, no parser/config value echo, private hidden-fragment absence, every refusal sentinel no-write/no-echo, accepted warning/override honesty, migration state and byte-copy rollback, v0.1 parse-only compatibility, committed exposure lane, warning/set ordering, doctor mismatch findings, and no contract drift. Scanner benchmark is >=10000 maximum-payload iterations, p95 <=5ms and max <=20ms on recorded host. Multi-project remains outside this gate. No cargo publish as papercuts; pilot uses exact SHA and exact isolated path only.

title: Design a reversible allowlisted pilot and installation lane
description:
## Background
Wider rollout must follow evidence from a small pilot. Candidate projects are papercuts, acfs-workbench, and at most two other active repositories. The forked binary must be unambiguous and reversible.

## Work
Choose installation/version naming, allowlisted repositories, private log locations/profiles, canonical AGENTS snippets, observation period, success/failure metrics, support/rollback steps, and evidence retention. Prevent PATH ambiguity with upstream v0.1.0. Define how read-only tasks report friction without mutation.

## Acceptance
The pilot plan names exact repos and owners, commands for install/preflight/verification/rollback, metrics for useful cuts/noise/false positives/leaks, stop conditions, and a bounded duration. No repository is modified by this planning bead.
acceptance_criteria: Allowlist максимум из четырёх проектов, versioned install, metrics, duration, stop conditions, rollback и evidence retention указаны; planning bead не меняет целевые repos.
notes:
Pilot naming rule copied from x30.6. Install the exact reviewed fork SHA under an isolated root inside HOME local opt papercuts-fork, invoke its bin papercuts by exact path, and do not put it ahead of upstream papercuts on global PATH. Cargo package and binary may remain papercuts only for this isolated pilot. Record full SHA and uninstall and rollback path. If fork-only behavior remains, both names must change before non-isolated public distribution. Canonical commands and policy: docs/UPSTREAM_SYNC_AND_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md section 8.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Pilot design must keep default private balanced behavior, prohibit autonomous override use, identify where warning/override/legacy-unscanned records are reviewed, and include a rapid binary rollback that does not rewrite journals. Pilot evidence must not contain detected values.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Pilot binary must implement machine contract 2 and pass x30.13. Install exact reviewed SHA in isolated HOME local opt root, invoke by exact path, never shadow upstream PATH, and keep package/binary name papercuts only for this isolated pilot. Default profile private/balanced; committed/strict is explicit exposure. Pilot plan must cover read-only/no-override AGENTS policy, migration state, warnings/legacy-unscanned review, before/after git status, stop-on-disclosure, sanitized evidence, uninstall and selection-only rollback without journal rewrite.

title: Execute the allowlisted pilot and collect sanitized evidence
description:
## Outcome
Run the approved hardened binary in the allowlisted projects long enough to learn whether it captures useful friction without creating security, noise, or worktree problems.

## Work
Apply only approved instructions/configuration, verify each repo before and after, collect counts and categories rather than raw private content where possible, exercise review/resolve/rollback, and record false positives, misses, command friction, agent compliance, and operator time. Do not widen beyond the allowlist.

## Evidence
Maintain a sanitized ledger with binary SHA/version, repo key, profile, test dates, counts, failures, mitigations, and rollback results. Any suspected disclosure stops the pilot and triggers cleanup/incident handling outside public logs.

## Acceptance
All pilot scenarios complete or stop safely; no unexplained repository mutation or sensitive publication; evidence is sufficient for a go/no-go decision; every change has a verified rollback.
acceptance_criteria: Каждый allowlisted repo имеет before/after и rollback proof; собраны sanitized signal/noise/false-positive/operator-cost данные; scope не расширен; disclosure немедленно останавливает pilot.
notes:
Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Collect sanitized counts by policy decision/category only; never copy matched values, offsets, hashes or raw records into pilot evidence. Exercise at least one synthetic refusal, balanced warning, strict refusal, authorized synthetic override, and legacy-unscanned readback while preserving the operator authorization record outside Papercuts.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Run only the contract-2 exact SHA and approved allowlist. Record repository alias/key only as approved pilot evidence, never raw locators or papercut content. For each project prove before/after worktree state, private target/profile, agent instruction, useful/noise counts, policy decision/category counts, false positives/misses, operator time, review/resolve and rollback. Synthetic refusal/warn/strict/override/legacy cases are isolated from real content. Any forbidden publication or unexplained mutation stops the pilot.

title: Decide wider adoption from pilot evidence
description:
## Outcome
Make a documented go, revise-and-repeat, or stop decision using pilot evidence rather than enthusiasm.

## Analysis
Evaluate signal quality, duplicate/noise rate, false positives/known misses, agent compliance, read-only behavior, worktree impact, operator review cost, rollback reliability, and security observations. Decide whether to widen single-project use and whether multi-project review is justified.

## Acceptance
Decision cites sanitized evidence, defines residual risks and next scope, updates docs/worklog, and either authorizes the next phase or creates explicit remediation beads. No automatic global rollout occurs in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: На основе evidence принято go/revise/stop решение с residual risks; следующий scope или remediation beads явны; глобальный rollout не выполняется автоматически.
notes: Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Decision must separately answer: widen single-project contract-2 use; repeat/stop pilot; and whether pilot evidence authorizes x30.17 design work. It does not authorize inventory implementation, global rollout, or public release automatically. Evaluate relevant-env/read-only compliance, private target/worktree impact, balanced false positives, known misses, override governance, legacy risk, operator cost, rollback, and exact-SHA installation ambiguity.

title: Design an explicit allowlisted multi-project inventory contract
description:
## Background
Cross-project review can identify repeated friction, but unrestricted filesystem scanning would violate project boundaries and collect private evidence. Source journals must remain authoritative.

## Work
Define an explicit allowlist/inventory format, stable project keys, journal locations, aliases, failure isolation, retention, permissions, output bounds, and JSON/Markdown digest contract. Specify discovery that never traverses arbitrary roots, clustering that never rewrites journals, and safe handling of missing/corrupt/locked logs. Decide whether this is a separate binary/subcommand/thin adapter.

## Acceptance
Threat model, examples, schema, compatibility, preflight, rollback, and unit/e2e cases are explicit. ACFS/ClickUp/CM details stay outside the generic core unless proven generic.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory строго allowlisted, не сканирует arbitrary roots, определяет project keys/aliases/bounds/errors/retention/schema/rollback и полный privacy-focused test plan.
notes:
Project identity decision copied from x30.3; canonical detail: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md section 7. Source records contain no project key, path hash, remote, basename, UUID or machine ID. Inventory privately maps a human alias to one resolved journal and emits alias only in digest. Alias is 1 to 64 lowercase ASCII; first alphanumeric; remaining alphanumeric dot underscore hyphen; dot and dot-dot forbidden; no separators/whitespace/URL/normalization. Aliases unique and one canonical journal cannot appear twice. Linked worktrees share one source; separate clone stays separate until operator decision. Locator remains private; no recursive scan or remote comparison.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Inventory/digest contract must treat content_policy as a trust boundary. By default exclude warn, override and legacy-unscanned records from aggregate content, or require a separate explicit operator choice; never treat absence of a finding as proof that a record is safe.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Contract 2 deliberately contains no multi-project command. This design starts only if x30.16 authorizes it. Fixed boundary: explicit private allowlist is sole discovery authority; no recursive scan/remote lookup; alias 1-64 lowercase ASCII with exact grammar, unique and one per canonical journal; linked worktrees one source, clones separate; source read-only; digest emits alias never locator/path/hash/remote/machine ID; warn/override/legacy-unscanned excluded by default absent a separately approved inclusion gate; no relabel-clean or source resolution. Decide separate adapter/command only inside these limits.

title: Implement allowlisted multi-project digest with bounded output
description:
## Outcome
Produce deterministic JSON and Markdown review digests from only explicitly configured project journals while keeping each source journal authoritative and unchanged.

## Scope
Implement approved inventory parsing, alias/project-key handling, bounded concurrency/output, per-project error isolation, status/tag/severity filters, deterministic ordering, and sanitized diagnostics. No arbitrary recursive scanning, network sync, journal rewriting, or automatic resolution.

## Tests and evidence
Unit-test inventory validation and stable ordering; e2e multiple disposable repos including missing, corrupt, locked, private, and mixed-version logs; prove no unlisted path is opened; log detailed sanitized phases and cleanup. Document preflight, verification, rollback, and evidence.

## Acceptance
Outputs match schema; sources remain byte-identical; failures are isolated and machine-readable; privacy boundaries are tested; cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Prefer an isolated inventory/digest module or thin separate command surface. Reuse read/fold APIs without widening default repo discovery. Source journals are read-only inputs; inventory configuration is the only discovery authority.
acceptance_criteria: Unlisted paths не открываются; source journals byte-identical; deterministic bounded JSON/Markdown digest и per-project failure isolation покрыты e2e; cargo gate и UBS зелёные.
notes:
Implementation identity contract from x30.3: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md section 7. Inventory config is sole discovery authority and privately maps validated alias to journal locator. Output adds alias only, never locator/path/hash/remote. Reject duplicate alias and same canonical journal under multiple aliases; do not normalize. Worktrees sharing common Git dir are one source; clones are independent. Preserve source bytes and apply private safe projection to contract1/legacy records before JSON/Markdown. No unlisted path open, directory scan, remote lookup or source write.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Default digest must exclude warn, override and legacy-unscanned content, or implement the separately approved explicit inclusion gate from x30.17. Do not rescan and relabel old content clean; propagate category-only policy state without raw values in diagnostics or evidence.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Implement only the x30.17-approved surface; do not widen generic contract-2 single-project discovery. Reuse read/fold/private projection read-only, preserve source bytes, and treat inventory config as sole locator source. Default exclude warn, override and legacy-unscanned content; any inclusion control must be explicit, bounded, audited and already approved by x30.17. Output and errors contain alias/category/status only, never locator or raw suspect diagnostics. Tests prove no unlisted open and deterministic bounded ordering.

title: Define and pilot promotion rules for issues, runbooks, CM, and ClickUp
description:
## Background
A papercut journal is useful only if repeated friction can become durable improvement work. Destinations have different semantics: repository bugs/tasks, runbooks/helpers, cross-agent CM rules, and human project work in ClickUp. Automatic writeback would be dangerous.

## Work
Define evidence thresholds, deduplication, human approval, destination selection, reference recording on resolution, failure/rollback, and idempotency. Start with payload generation or thin adapters; no automatic bulk writes. Keep ACFS/Beads/ClickUp/CM integration outside the generic Rust core unless it is universally applicable.

## Tests
Use dry-run/payload-only fixtures first. For any live connector pilot, require explicit scope, one-record write, readback, cleanup/rollback, and no secrets. Verify a successful API response with independent readback.

## Acceptance
A concise decision/runbook exists; only adapters justified by repeated pilot evidence are implemented; unresolved or declined suggestions remain traceable without rewriting source journals. This bead does not block the first generic public release unless the release scope explicitly includes adapters.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence thresholds, approval, dedup, dry-run payloads, readback and rollback определены; live writes только one-record and verified; generic core не загрязнён ACFS-specific logic.
notes: Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Contract-2 resolution notes are maximum 2000 UTF-8 bytes, scanned, append-only, and may be private content. Promotion references must avoid raw private URLs/data in public journals. Generic Rust core must not perform ClickUp/Beads/CM writes. Any adapter begins payload-only/dry-run, requires explicit human scope, idempotency key, one-record write, independent readback and rollback; source cut resolves only after verified durable improvement.

title: Decide safe storage profiles and read-only task semantics
description:
## Background
Upstream discovers a repository and writes .papercuts.jsonl there by default. That makes friction visible in diffs, but it can dirty repositories and conflicts with audit-only/read-only work. The fork needs safe defaults without pretending a CLI can infer every conversational authorization boundary.

## Work
Define named or flag-based storage profiles, precedence across flag/environment/defaults, first-run behavior, repository-local versus user-state locations, and an explicit legacy compatibility path. Decide what the CLI can enforce versus what AGENTS.md policy must enforce. Specify whether read-only commands may discover without creating directories and how mutation attempts should fail or warn under a restricted profile.

## Required analysis
Compare private-by-default, committed-by-default with warnings, and explicit-profile-only designs. Cover existing logs, non-git directories, worktrees/submodules, missing HOME, permission errors, and migration rollback. No network or persistent daemon.

## Acceptance
An ADR-quality decision selects one default and one migration story; precedence and failure behavior are unambiguous; schema implications and black-box test cases are enumerated; no fake guarantee is made that the CLI knows a task was declared read-only.
acceptance_criteria: Выбран один safe default и explicit legacy mode; precedence, no-write semantics, migration, rollback и black-box cases зафиксированы; CLI не приписывается способность понимать conversational read-only scope.
notes:
Canonical decision: docs/SAFE_STORAGE_PROFILES_ADR.md dated 2026-07-11. Product code is unchanged.
Profiles: private default; committed legacy storage compatibility; custom for flag or environment file. Explicit target precedence is flag file, environment file, flag profile, environment profile, then private default.
Private valid-Git target: validated common Git directory plus papercuts/log.jsonl. Linked worktrees share it; submodules use their own common dir. Outside validated Git, implicit private mutation is storage_required exit 78 and requires explicit file. Committed valid-Git target is root .papercuts.jsonl; committed non-Git target is HOME .papercuts/log.jsonl.
Read-only guard: CLI flag OR true environment value; deny wins and no CLI negation exists. Actual add and resolve return writes_disabled 78 before stdin, clock, discovery or I/O. Dry-runs remain no-write previews. AGENTS policy still decides conversational authority and must not call mutations in read-only tasks.
Upgrade: legacy-only state warns on reads, permits add dry-run preview, and refuses resolve dry-run plus actual mutations with migration_required 78. No auto-copy, move, merge, delete, gitignore or fallback. Migration is stop writers, doctor source, copy bytes to absent private target with restrictive permissions, compare, doctor target, retain source. Rollback selects committed and retains private.
Private Unix create modes are 0700 directory and 0600 file. Insecure existing modes produce doctor finding and append refusal. New stable errors include storage_required 78, migration_required 78, writes_disabled 78 and insecure_private_permissions 77. Metadata exposes storage_profile, storage_source and write_policy. Hardened contract must be at least 2; contract-1 journals remain readable.
Review: three passes covered source consistency, adversarial operations, and implementation readiness. Disposable probes confirmed main plus linked worktree share a common dir and submodule common dir is distinct.
Verification: release build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy denied warnings PASS; fmt and diff check PASS; doctor healthy with 8 lines; gitleaks no leaks across 13 commits; UBS skipped for docs, planning, Beads and append-only raw-journal-only changes.
close_reason: Accepted ADR selects private Git-common-dir default, committed legacy mode, explicit non-Git storage, monotonic read-only guard, migration and rollback state machine, permissions, schema/errors, and complete test matrix; downstream context propagated and full docs-only gate passed.

title: Prepare and publish the first hardened public fork release
description:
## Outcome
Publish a transparent hardened fork release only after the approved functional phases and public safety gates pass.

## Work
Settle version/name/install method; synchronize or document upstream divergence; update compatibility matrix, README, migration and rollback instructions, changelog, attribution, and release notes. Do not publish to crates.io or another namespace without verified ownership and explicit decision. Clearly distinguish upstream behavior, implemented fork hardening, optional multi-project capability, and deferred adapters.

## Release gate
Run full Rust quality gate, adversarial/e2e suite, doctor, UBS, dependency review, license/attribution audit, and gitleaks over public history and artifacts. Verify clean checkout installation and smoke lifecycle. Record commit/tag/artifact hashes and rollback.

## Acceptance
Public GitHub release is reproducible, contains no private operational material, makes no unsupported security claim, links upstream, documents compatibility and known limitations, and has a tested uninstall/rollback path.
acceptance_criteria: Clean-checkout install и smoke lifecycle воспроизводимы; cargo/e2e/UBS/dependency/license/gitleaks gates зелёные; tag/artifact hashes записаны; attribution, limitations, migration and rollback опубликованы.
notes:
Public-release policy copied from x30.6. Canonical runbook: docs/UPSTREAM_SYNC_AND_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md. Repository remains GitHub fork named papercuts and preserves MIT and upstream attribution. If any fork-only default, record shape, input acceptance, discovery, or contract behavior remains, rename Cargo package and installed binary before non-isolated public distribution and prove coexistence. Do not publish crates.io papercuts; upstream 0.1.0 occupies the namespace and authority is unverified. First breaking prerelease starts no lower than 0.2.0-alpha.1 with a hardened-v SemVer tag. GitHub-only until a distinct registry name and publishing authority are verified and user-approved. Release notes must say community-maintained fork, not upstream release, and record upstream tag and full SHA, fork SHA, compatibility, carried patches, gates, limitations, and rollback.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Release notes must identify the behavioral break: new field and size bounds, private balanced and committed strict floors, category-only two-key override, sensitive_input exit 65, content_policy audit, and legacy-unscanned history. State explicit misses and that accepted warnings/overrides persist original input; do not claim exhaustive detection.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Release must publish the exact machine contract 2 and enumerate changed default storage, strict Git validation, omitted/legacy path fields, relevant-env/lazy-clock change, new field bounds/content_policy, parser/error redaction, new errors, private balanced and committed strict floors, migration, v0.1 parse-only compatibility, and known scanner/path limits. Because fork-only behavior remains, rename Cargo package and binary before non-isolated distribution and prove coexistence. GitHub repo remains papercuts; tag hardened-v at no lower than 0.2.0-alpha.1; never publish crates.io papercuts; preserve MIT/upstream attribution and immutable history.

title: Define tag vocabulary and review lifecycle
description:
## Background
The pilot must turn raw cuts into a manageable review practice. Free-form tags fragment easily, duplicate cuts can hide recurring friction, and resolution without a durable reference makes it hard to prove improvement. The project plan calls for configurable tag vocabulary, review cadence, and promotion references.

## Work
Using sanitized pilot evidence, define a small canonical tag taxonomy with extension rules; severity guidance; duplicate/recurrence handling; review cadence; states for fixed, declined, superseded, or promoted outcomes; and how resolution notes reference a Bead, issue, runbook, commit, CM rule, or ClickUp workstream without embedding private URLs/data in public journals. Decide which behavior belongs in generic CLI schema versus operator documentation or adapters.

## Tests and evidence
Replay representative synthetic/pilot-derived categories through list/digest workflows. Verify unknown/custom tags remain forward-compatible, deterministic sorting survives, and no taxonomy migration rewrites append-only history.

## Acceptance
The lifecycle is small enough for daily use, distinguishes papercuts from bugs/incidents/completed work, supports local extensions, defines review and evidence rules, and provides migration/rollback without journal rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical tags, extension rules, severity, duplicate handling, review cadence and resolution references are evidence-backed, deterministic and append-only compatible; boundaries to bugs/incidents/worklogs are explicit.
notes:
Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Tag design is constrained by policy v1: maximum 16 tags, 64 UTF-8 bytes each, every tag scanned as field tag, sorted/deduped policy categories and fields. Vocabulary examples must avoid token-shaped literals; validation and scanner ordering must stay deterministic.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Contract 2 fixes tag mechanics before taxonomy: at most 16 tags, each at most 64 UTF-8 bytes, sorted for storage/ID, each scanned as field tag, and policy category/field arrays sorted separately. Taxonomy changes cannot rewrite old events or alter existing IDs. Resolution references are bounded/scanned and must not embed private locators. This lifecycle follows pilot evidence and cannot change the machine contract silently.

title: Decide path minimization and repository identity contract
description:
## Background
Upstream cut records capture absolute cwd and repo paths. These can reveal usernames and internal layouts when journals are committed. Multi-project review also needs a stable identity without unrestricted filesystem discovery.

## Work
Choose among omission, repository-relative paths, stable project keys, salted hashes, or explicit modes. Specify defaults, compatibility, serialization shape, symlink/worktree behavior, paths outside the repo, non-UTF8 handling, and whether existing contract-1 readers tolerate the change. Define how a later allowlisted inventory identifies projects without embedding sensitive absolute paths in public records.

## Security constraints
Safe mode must minimize metadata before content scanning. Errors and warnings must not echo a path the selected policy intended to hide. Do not create a reversible fingerprint that merely disguises a private path.

## Acceptance
The decision includes exact example records for safe and legacy modes, collision/privacy analysis, schema/version consequences, migration and rollback, and focused unit plus black-box tests.
acceptance_criteria: Есть точные safe/legacy record examples, privacy and collision analysis, symlink/worktree rules, schema/version impact, migration, rollback и тестовая матрица.
notes:
Canonical decision: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md dated 2026-07-12. Product code unchanged.
Private new record retains parser-compatible cwd dot and repo null, adds path_policy omitted and path_encoding omitted. Committed new record retains absolute cwd/repo plus path_policy legacy-absolute and encoding utf8 or lossy-utf8. Fields and paths stay outside ID; exact shared example ID pc_94f5df71022d.
Profile remains private or committed when file flag or env overrides target. Metadata separates profile_source from storage_source. Private meta omits file and every cut output projects old/legacy paths to safe sentinels without rewriting source. Committed explicitly opts into legacy record/meta/error path exposure.
Safe errors never interpolate Path display, lossy path or raw OS error text; they use opaque locations. Markdown stays path-free. Agent-authored text, tags, notes and aliases remain content-risk inputs for x30.4.
Project identity is external: later private allowlist maps validated human alias to one journal and digest emits alias only. No record project field, path hash, remote, basename, UUID or machine ID. Alias is 1 to 64 lowercase ASCII with alphanumeric first and alphanumeric/dot/underscore/hyphen remainder; dot and dot-dot forbidden; unique alias and journal.
Strict resolver: physical cwd, nearest marker; malformed nearest marker errors rather than outer inheritance. Direct .git symlink rejected. Ordinary marker validates HEAD and common config/objects. Gitdir and commondir files are one LF/CRLF line, absolute or relative, no NUL/extra content. Worktree/submodule supported, no refs-dir or Git-binary requirement. Private final journal symlink rejected. Platform-native paths avoid safe UTF8 conversion.
Migration copies old bytes without sanitizing; private output redacts, committed restores stored values, no rewrite. Unchanged v0.1 parsed and doctored the exact safe record; this proves parse compatibility only.
Three review passes: surface inventory, adversarial privacy, compatibility/implementation readiness.
Verification: build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy denied warnings PASS; fmt/diff check PASS; doctor healthy 9 lines; gitleaks no leaks 14 commits; UBS skipped for docs/planning/Beads/raw-journal only.
close_reason: Accepted ADR defines exact safe and legacy record shapes, safe projection and diagnostics, strict Git/worktree/symlink/non-UTF8 behavior, external allowlisted project identity, compatibility, migration, rollback and exhaustive tests; downstream context propagated and full docs-only gate passed.

title: Define deterministic sensitive-data guardrail behavior
description:
## Background
Cut text is agent-authored free form. A token, credential URL, key fragment, email, customer identifier, or raw config could be appended and pushed. Detection is a guardrail, never proof of safety.

## Work
Define a bounded, local, deterministic preflight: threat classes, pattern sources, entropy use if any, allowlist/suppression rules, size limits, warn-versus-refuse policy, override semantics, dry-run behavior, redacted diagnostics, and structured error/schema contract. Decide how to avoid echoing the suspected secret and how to explain false positives without exposing values.

## Required adversarial cases
Bearer/basic credentials, common cloud/token prefixes, PEM material, credential-bearing URLs, emails/identifiers, benign hashes, test fixtures, shell snippets, Unicode, multiline input, very long input, and encoded material. Explicitly document misses that remain possible.

## Acceptance
A reviewed policy matrix and test corpus specification exist; default behavior is selected; override is deliberate and auditable; no raw suspect value appears in output/logs; performance and offline constraints are stated.
acceptance_criteria: Policy matrix определяет warn/refuse/override, redacted diagnostics, bounded offline checks, false-positive controls, synthetic corpus и честные limitations; подозрительное значение нигде не эхоится.
notes:
Baseline audit: docs/UPSTREAM_V0_1_COMPATIBILITY_AUDIT.md.
Facts to carry into this decision: add accepts any non-empty UTF-8 text up to 10,000 bytes and persists it unchanged; there is no credential/PII/path preflight; default storage is a tracked repo file; error/schema outputs are structured and deterministic. A default refusal of previously accepted text is a behavioral break and needs an explicit hardened-version plus legacy/override story. Suspect values must never be echoed.

Decision source: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md dated 2026-07-12.
Default: private profile floor balanced; committed floor strict. Requested policy precedence is flag, env, profile floor; no off and no downgrade.
Scanner: local deterministic policy v1 over cut text, tags, persisted agent, and resolve note. Bounds are text 10000 bytes with stdin cap 10001, note 2000, tag 64 x16, agent 128, total 16384, patterns <=128.
High categories always refuse: private_key, authorization_header, credential_url, secret_assignment, github_token, slack_token, stripe_secret_key, aws_credential_pair. Medium categories warn in balanced and refuse in strict: email_address, personal_identifier, filesystem_path, config_block.
Override: truthy PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE plus exact repeated --allow-sensitive on add/resolve for every refusing category. No wildcard, partial, unknown, flag-only, or unused preauthorization. Canonical AGENTS policy forbids autonomous agent override.
Audit: new cut/resolve content_policy stores version, mode, clean/warn/override, sorted categories and fields; excluded from ID. Old records are legacy-unscanned and never rewritten.
Errors: sensitive_input exit 65. Refusal output, logs, files and artifacts contain no value, fragment, context, position, length, pattern, hash or path. Accepted warn/override is not redaction and persists normal record content.
Limits: no entropy, recursive decoding, Unicode normalization, network, subprocess, runtime catalog or exhaustive guarantee. Split/encoded/unmarked material may pass.
Verification: release build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS; fmt/diff PASS; doctor healthy 12 lines; gitleaks no leaks across 15 commits; graph no cycles; UBS skipped for docs/planning/Beads/journal-only changes. Decisions propagated to affected downstream beads.
close_reason: Reviewed deterministic guardrail policy matrix, bounded offline catalog, two-key category override, redacted refusal contract, dry-run behavior, content-policy audit, adversarial corpus, performance budget, honest limitations, and downstream propagation are complete and verified.

title: Publish the hardened contract ADR and converge the plan
description:
## Outcome
Integrate the approved storage, path, and sensitive-data decisions into one self-contained hardened contract before implementation.

## Work
Update the project plan and add an ADR that defines user workflows, CLI/profile resolution, record metadata, schema/version policy, threat model, compatibility mode, rollback, and ownership boundaries between Rust mechanism and AGENTS.md policy. Reconcile contradictions with the upstream normative design explicitly instead of silently editing promises. Include concrete command and JSON examples plus rejected alternatives and rationale.

## Review protocol
Perform multiple fresh-context review passes until changes become incremental. Check usability, security honesty, deterministic behavior, append-only invariants, and migration. Separate generic upstream candidates from fork-only policy.

## Beads propagation gate
Before closing this bead, use br update to copy the approved default, exact flags/env precedence, record examples, error/version behavior, rollback, and forbidden actions into every affected implementation/test/docs bead. A downstream agent must not need PROJECT_PLAN.md or chat history to learn the final decisions.

## Acceptance
Every downstream implementation bead can cite an exact decision contained in its own copied context; all eight open decisions from the project plan are resolved or deliberately deferred with a gate; no broad coding is authorized while this bead remains open.
acceptance_criteria: ADR разрешает или явно откладывает все восемь open decisions, согласован с upstream invariants, содержит rejected alternatives и разрешает downstream implementation только после нескольких review passes.
notes:
Upstream and naming decision copied from x30.6. Canonical detail: docs/UPSTREAM_SYNC_AND_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md. ADR must preserve public main without rebase, reset, or force-push; keep generic upstream fixes isolated from fork policy and ACFS adapters. GitHub repo stays papercuts; rename package and binary before non-isolated distribution if fork-only behavior remains; do not publish crates.io papercuts.
Storage decision copied from x30.2. Canonical detail: docs/SAFE_STORAGE_PROFILES_ADR.md. Private default uses validated Git common-dir papercuts/log.jsonl; committed is legacy; non-Git private writes need explicit file; read-only guard denies appends; no auto-migration; contract exactly 2.
Path decision copied from x30.3. Canonical detail: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. Private records keep parser-compatible cwd dot and repo null plus path_policy omitted and path_encoding omitted; committed records retain labeled legacy absolute paths. Private output redacts old stored paths without rewriting bytes. Explicit file changes target only, so selected private or committed profile still controls projection. Project identity is external operator alias in an allowlist, never record path/hash/remote/basename/UUID. Consolidated ADR must reconcile profile_source and storage_source, strict Git resolver, safe diagnostics, legacy parse compatibility and sensitive text policy before implementation.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Consolidate private floor balanced and committed floor strict; policy flag then env then profile floor, with weaker-than-floor config_error. Preserve exact two-key category override, bounded all-field preflight, content_policy audit, sensitive_input exit 65, legacy-unscanned semantics, and contract exactly 2. Reconcile read-only and logical target resolution before bounded stdin, then scanner before journal create/open/lock/read, duplicate lookup, clock and ID.

Final decision source: docs/HARDENED_CONTRACT_ADR.md dated 2026-07-12.
Architecture gate: machine contract exactly 2; current product remains upstream v0.1 until implementation evidence exists.
Eight decisions: private default; committed explicit; private balanced/committed strict content floors; bounded offline policy v1; private paths omitted with v1-compatible sentinels; common-Git-dir private target and explicit non-Git target; development/exact-SHA pilot name retention with pre-distribution rename gate; generic upstream fixes isolated; multi-project interface deferred after pilot to x30.17.
Unified surface: globals --profile, --read-only, --sensitive-policy plus existing --file/--pretty; add/resolve repeated --allow-sensitive; new PROFILE, READ_ONLY, SENSITIVE_POLICY, ALLOW_SENSITIVE env. Schema ignores ambient env; other commands read only relevant env; textual values require UTF-8 while FILE/HOME stay native; clock is lazy.
Order: validate relevant non-clock config, deny actual read-only mutation, validate non-consuming args, resolve logical target/migration without create, bound and scan input, refuse before clock/record/duplicate/I-O, then preserve exclusive read-fold-decide-append.
Storage/path: private validated common-dir papercuts/log.jsonl with 0700/0600; private non-Git requires explicit; committed repo or HOME; explicit file changes target only; implicit migration state only private profile-default; no auto-copy/merge/fallback. One strict native Git resolver; private cwd dot/repo null/path_policy omitted/path_encoding omitted; committed legacy-absolute with encoding; private output redacts old paths without rewrite.
Content: exact bounds and 12-category policy v1; two-key exact override; new cut/resolve content_policy version/mode/decision/categories/fields excluded from ID; old absence is legacy-unscanned. sensitive_input exit 65 never echoes or writes refused value; accepted warn/override persists input honestly.
Contract/errors: exact private and committed example ID pc_94f5df71022d. Add/resolve full policy meta; list/doctor omit sensitive-policy meta; private omits file; warnings/set arrays sorted. New stable errors include sensitive_input, insecure_private_permissions, writes_disabled, storage_required, migration_required, invalid_repository, unsupported_filesystem, unsafe_journal_symlink. Parser/config/ID/since errors do not echo rejected values. v0.1 can parse records but is not contract-2 compatible; committed is not unchecked.
Gates: migration is explicit byte-copy/verify with source retained; rollback is selection-only or exact upstream binary; public history uses revert, never rewrite. Package/binary rename before non-isolated distribution while fork-only behavior remains; never publish crates.io papercuts.
Polish: x30.7-.13 descriptions/acceptance rewritten; final contract copied to x30.7-.21; x30.11 multi-project scope creep removed; all Beads self-contained, graph acyclic.
Verification: release build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS; fmt/diff PASS; doctor healthy 15 lines; gitleaks no leaks across 16 commits; product code identical to upstream/main; UBS skipped for planning/docs/Beads/journal-only changes.
close_reason: Normative hardened contract 2, all eight planning decisions, exact CLI/records/errors/migration/rollback/ownership, six review passes, project-state convergence, and self-contained downstream Bead propagation are complete and verified.

title: Define upstream contribution, naming, and synchronization strategy
description:
## Background
The repository is a real GitHub fork and must preserve MIT attribution and an understandable relationship to treygoff24/papercuts. Generic fixes should be separable from ACFS-specific adapters. The crates.io name and binary compatibility cannot be assumed.

## Work
Define remote-sync procedure, upstream tag/fork compatibility matrix, commit separation rules, criteria for opening upstream PRs, policy for carrying rejected changes, fork naming/version/package strategy, and release notes language. Cover how to fetch/rebase or merge without rewriting public history and how to recover from upstream divergence.

## Acceptance
A maintainer runbook provides preflight, sync commands, conflict handling, verification, rollback, and evidence. The plan clearly states when the binary/crate keeps or changes its name and prevents accidental publication to a package namespace we do not control.
acceptance_criteria: Есть runbook sync/PR/divergence, package/name policy, compatibility matrix и rollback; MIT attribution сохранена; запрещена публикация в неподтверждённый namespace.
notes:
Decision source: docs/UPSTREAM_SYNC_AND_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md dated 2026-07-11.
Snapshot: upstream/main ffba2bd453ab0faeadf4f923fc727586958c8d6f; v0.1.0 5d8b827abbd054f5f506d26be865f5b7f573a298; fork pre-decision 6e7dd774778866821e6969779772d02e18d572c1; origin was 4 ahead and 0 behind.
Policy: public main is never rebased, reset, or force-pushed. Fetch-only upstream uses a DISABLED push sentinel. Integrate upstream through dated sync branches and merge-commit fork PRs; roll back published merges with revert PRs.
Upstream candidates: risk docs, rust-version and MSRV docs plus CI, dangling absolute symlink cleanup. Fork-only unless accepted upstream: safe default, path minimization, sensitive-input refusal, strict repo validation, ACFS adapters.
Naming: GitHub repo stays papercuts. Cargo package and binary may stay papercuts only through development and exact-SHA isolated pilot. Rename both before non-isolated public distribution if fork-only behavior remains.
Registry: official sparse index contains upstream papercuts 0.1.0; owner API returned HTTP 503. No cargo publish or registry workflow under papercuts without explicit verified authority. First fork release is GitHub-only and uses a hardened-v SemVer tag; breaking prerelease starts no lower than 0.2.0-alpha.1.
Verification: build PASS; 30 tests PASS; clippy with denied warnings PASS; fmt and diff check PASS; doctor healthy; gitleaks no leaks; UBS skipped for docs, planning, and raw-journal-only slice.
close_reason: Maintainer runbook, compatibility matrix, upstream PR and divergence policy, immutable-history sync and rollback, MIT attribution, package and binary rename boundary, registry prohibition, and downstream decision propagation are complete and verified.

title: Implement safe profile and storage resolution
description:
## Outcome
Implement the contract-2 typed policy and storage seam: private default, explicit committed profile, monotonic read-only guard, relevant-environment handling, lazy clock boundary, target precedence, and migration state machine.

## Scope
Add --profile, --read-only, and --sensitive-policy global parsing plus the new environment parsing required by the shared policy context. Resolve profile_source independently from storage_source. Implement private Git-common-dir and non-Git explicit-target behavior, committed repository/HOME targets, implicit legacy detection, no-fallback errors, private permissions, and no-create read/dry-run behavior. Provide stable interfaces consumed by path and sensitive-policy Beads. Do not implement the scanner catalog or path projection here. Do not refactor fold/locking.

## Tests and evidence
Table-test flag/env/default precedence, textual versus native path environment values, command-relevant env reads, schema no-env behavior, lazy PAPERCUTS_NOW reads, read-only deny ordering, Git/non-Git first run, explicit targets, missing HOME, legacy-only and dual-journal state, permissions, symlink handoff, no fallback, and exact side effects. Add black-box tests in disposable roots and keep schema truthful for every surface implemented by this slice.

## Acceptance
One typed policy context drives all commands without duplicate environment reads; private and committed targets, metadata sources, read-only semantics, lazy-clock/relevant-env behavior, migration state, permissions, errors, and rollback match contract 2; no read/dry-run creates forbidden state; upstream fold/lock invariants, full cargo gate, and scoped UBS pass.
design: Own CLI/config parsing, shared policy types, and storage discovery/state interfaces. Coordinate src/cli.rs, src/commands/mod.rs, src/store.rs, src/output.rs, and tests. Expose stable hooks for x30.8 and x30.9; update schema incrementally so the checked-in binary never lies. Do not implement path projection, scanner patterns, or broad schema consolidation assigned later.
acceptance_criteria: Typed contract-2 policy context, private/committed storage, relevant-env/lazy-clock behavior, monotonic read-only guard, migration/no-fallback state and exact side effects are unit/black-box tested; fold/locking unchanged; cargo gate and UBS pass.
notes:
Approved storage and path input: docs/SAFE_STORAGE_PROFILES_ADR.md plus docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. Profiles are only private and committed. Resolve profile_source independently from storage_source: profile flag then env then private default; file flag then env then profile-default target. File override never erases profile. Private valid-Git target is common-dir papercuts/log.jsonl; private non-Git mutation needs explicit file; committed uses legacy root or HOME target. Read-only deny wins before input and I/O. Legacy-only migration refuses real mutation; no auto-copy or fallback. Return one typed policy including profile, profile_source, storage_source, write_policy and path_policy for every command.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. The shared typed policy result must also resolve sensitive policy: --sensitive-policy then PAPERCUTS_SENSITIVE_POLICY then profile floor; private floor balanced, committed floor strict, never off or below floor. Validate PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE boolean and category flags centrally. Logical target resolution may inspect metadata before bounded input; no create/open/lock/read occurs until scanner acceptance.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Machine contract is exactly 2. Public globals are --profile private|committed, --read-only, --sensitive-policy balanced|strict plus existing --file/--pretty. Profile precedence flag, env, private default; target precedence file flag, file env, profile default; explicit file never changes profile. Read-only is flag OR env and actual mutation refuses before ID/input, clock, repo/migration, or journal probes after relevant config validation. Schema ignores ambient env. List/doctor read profile/file/read-only only. Add/resolve read all applicable env; list reads PAPERCUTS_NOW only for relative --since. Text env must be UTF-8, while PAPERCUTS_FILE and HOME retain native path encoding. Private Git target is common-dir papercuts/log.jsonl with 0700/0600 implicit state; private non-Git mutation needs explicit file. Committed uses validated repo .papercuts.jsonl or HOME. Implicit migration state applies only private profile-default; explicit file bypasses it. Add dry-run legacy-only previews; resolve dry-run refuses. New stable codes owned here include writes_disabled, storage_required, migration_required, insecure_private_permissions, unsupported_filesystem; never fall back. Keep schema incrementally truthful and fold/locking untouched.
close_reason: Implemented and verified typed contract-2 profile/storage policy, monotonic read-only guard, private/committed target resolution, migration states, permissions, symlink refusal, lazy environment handling, transitional schema, and black-box coverage. Full cargo gate, five repeated suites, gitleaks, and focused cross-review completed.

title: Implement privacy-preserving path and project metadata
description:
## Outcome
Implement contract-2 strict Git resolution, private automatic path omission, committed legacy-absolute capture, safe output/error projection, and compatible mixed-journal reading.

## Scope
Build one platform-native resolver for ordinary repositories, linked worktrees, and submodules. Reject malformed nearest markers, invalid gitdir/commondir grammar, direct marker symlinks, and private final-journal symlinks without falling back. New private cuts use cwd dot, repo null, path_policy omitted, path_encoding omitted. New committed cuts use legacy-absolute plus utf8 or lossy-utf8. Project identity stays out of source records; multi-project alias implementation is deferred. Project every private command output and error without rewriting source bytes.

## Tests and evidence
Pin exact private and committed event bytes and identical ID. Cover add/duplicate/dry-run/list/resolve/already-resolved/doctor/errors, contract-1 and mixed records, ordinary/worktree/submodule/bare/non-Git repositories, invalid nested markers, gitdir/commondir line grammar, symlinks/traversal, native non-UTF-8 paths, no Git in PATH, safe parser/OS diagnostics, path-policy mismatch findings, and committed exposure warnings. Assert forbidden fragments are absent from private journal/stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance
Private automatic paths and diagnostics expose no forbidden fragment; committed behavior is explicitly labeled; exact records and ID match contract 2; old records remain readable and append-only; resolver/path edge cases and rollback are proven; cargo gate and scoped UBS pass.
design: Own the typed Git/path resolver, record path fields, path doctor validation, and profile projection. Reuse x30.7 policy/storage seam. Coordinate CutRecord/output/error surfaces with x30.9; do not implement multi-project aliases or change ID/fold/locking.
acceptance_criteria: Exact omitted and legacy-absolute records, strict resolver, safe projection, mixed-v1/v2 compatibility, non-UTF8/symlink/worktree cases and no-hidden-fragment assertions pass; journals are never rewritten; cargo gate and UBS pass.
notes:
Approved path contract: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. New private cut exact policy is cwd dot, repo null, path_policy omitted, path_encoding omitted. New committed cuts label legacy-absolute plus utf8 or lossy-utf8. Fields remain excluded from ID. Private projection redacts every stored contract-1 or legacy cut in add/list/resolve/doctor outputs without rewriting journal bytes. Build one platform-native resolver: canonical physical cwd, nearest marker; malformed nearest marker errors rather than inheriting outer repo; ordinary .git directory must have HEAD plus common objects and config; gitdir and commondir files are one exact LF/CRLF line, support relative/absolute targets and worktree/submodule, reject NUL/extra lines/symlink marker. Do not require refs directory or Git binary. Reject private final journal symlinks. Safe errors use opaque location codes and never Path display, lossy path or raw OS error text.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. New cut and resolve records carry content_policy {version:1, mode, decision, sorted categories, sorted fields}; it is excluded from cut ID. Private automatic path fields are omitted and never scanned. Coordinate record construction so content scan happens before path/event assembly and scanner refusal cannot return a duplicate legacy record.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Machine contract is exactly 2. One native resolver uses nearest physical marker; invalid nearest marker never inherits outer repo. Ordinary .git requires real non-symlink directory with HEAD, objects, config. Gitdir/commondir are one LF/CRLF logical line, relative or absolute, reject empty/NUL/extra lines/bad prefix/unreadable target; Git dir needs HEAD and common dir needs objects/config, not refs; no Git executable. Bare is non-Git. Reject private implicit directory/final symlink and private explicit final symlink; parent symlink allowed; committed explicit keeps OS behavior. New private cut order preserves v1 fields then cwd dot, repo null, path_policy omitted, path_encoding omitted, content_policy. Committed uses legacy-absolute and utf8/lossy-utf8. Private projects old paths on every output without rewrite and omits meta.file; committed warns and may expose. PAPERCUTS_FILE/HOME paths stay native. Private errors use opaque locations; rejected argv/env values and raw OS strings are never echoed. Add invalid_repository and unsafe_journal_symlink errors plus path_policy_mismatch doctor finding. No alias/digest implementation in this bead.

title: Implement local sensitive-data preflight with redacted diagnostics
description:
## Outcome
Implement policy-version-1 bounded local sensitive-data preflight and contract-2 content_policy audit without claiming exhaustive detection or exposing refused values.

## Scope
Implement the pure static scanner, balanced/strict profile floors, policy precedence, two-key category override, bounded stdin/fields, dry-run parity, sensitive_input error, and cut/resolve audit object. Scan add text, each tag, persisted flag/env agent, and resolution note. Refusal occurs before clock, event construction, duplicate lookup, or journal create/open/lock/read. Accepted warn/override content is deliberately persisted and is not redaction. Keep operation offline, deterministic, and bounded.

## Tests and evidence
Table-test every high/medium category, exact placeholders, variable references, benign hashes/UUIDs/IDs, vendor publishable controls, argv/stdin, each persisted field, cross-field AWS pair, profile floor/downgrade, full override matrix, Unicode/multiline/CRLF, invalid UTF-8, exact and plus-one bounds, encoded/split limitations, dry-run, duplicate legacy records, category/field ordering, no-echo/no-write sentinels, and release benchmark. Use synthetic fixtures only.

## Acceptance
Every policy-matrix cell and limitation is represented; refused values are absent from stdout, stderr, journal, filesystem delta, debug output, and artifacts; accepted warn/override audit is exact and deterministic; performance budget, cargo gate, and scoped UBS pass.
design: Own a pure sensitive policy module, bounded input reader, ContentPolicy model, add/resolve integration, and sensitive_input construction. Reuse x30.7 policy context and x30.8 record/output seams. Do not add runtime patterns, network/subprocess calls, entropy, decoding, or fold/lock changes.
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic corpus, profile floors, exact override, bounds, content_policy, dry-run, duplicate ordering and refused-value no-echo/no-write assertions pass; known misses remain explicit; benchmark, cargo gate and UBS pass.
notes:
Path-policy input: docs/PATH_AND_PROJECT_IDENTITY_ADR.md. Private automatic path fields are omitted before record assembly and are not scanner inputs. Scanner covers only agent-authored text, tags and resolution notes according to the sensitive ADR; do not claim path omission sanitizes text. Refusals and override diagnostics must use category-only safe errors and must not echo cwd, repo, file target, alias or detected value. Committed legacy automatic absolute fields are an explicit exposure lane and must not be advertised as protected by the content scanner.

Sensitive-data decision copied from x30.4. Canonical detail: docs/SENSITIVE_DATA_GUARDRAIL_ADR.md. Implement policy v1 exactly: private balanced, committed strict, no off or downgrade; high categories private_key, authorization_header, credential_url, secret_assignment, github_token, slack_token, stripe_secret_key, aws_credential_pair; medium categories email_address, personal_identifier, filesystem_path, config_block. Scan add text, every tag, persisted flag/env agent, and resolve note. Bounds: text 10000 bytes with stdin read cap 10001, note 2000, tag 64 x16, agent 128, total 16384, patterns <=128. Override requires truthy PAPERCUTS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE plus exact repeated --allow-sensitive on add/resolve for every refusing category; reject wildcard, partial, unknown, flags without gate, and unused preauthorization. Persist category/field-only content_policy. Add sensitive_input exit 65. Refusal must not echo/hash/log/write the value. No entropy, decoding, Unicode normalization, network, subprocess, runtime patterns, or exhaustive claim. Release benchmark: >=10000 max-payload iterations, p95 <=5ms and max <=20ms on recorded host.

Final consolidated contract copied from x30.5. Machine contract is exactly 2 and policy version 1. Add/resolve own repeated --allow-sensitive. Scanner order is after logical target/migration validation and bounded input, but before clock, event/ID, duplicate lookup, create/open/lock/read. Audit object is {version,mode,decision,categories,fields}; stable fields are text, tag, agent, resolution_note; arrays sorted/deduped; clean arrays empty; warn medium-only; override records every observed category/field; excluded from ID. Old missing audit means legacy-unscanned and is never synthesized or rewritten. Private floor balanced, committed floor strict; flag then env then floor, no downgrade/off. Gate plus exact categories required; reject unknown, wildcard, partial, flag-only and unused preauthorization. Bounds and category catalog in existing note are normative. Refusal sensitive_input exit 65 contains category/field only and no match/context/position/length/pattern/hash. Accepted warn/override returns and stores original content. Known encoded/split/Unicode/vendor misses remain. Pure patterns <=128, payload <=16384; benchmark >=10000 max-input iterations with p95 <=5ms and max <=20ms on recorded release host.
